title: Shin Eva A new art style towards the ending
display_title: Shin Eva A new art style towards the ending
wikitext:
[]: Shin Eva: A new art style towards the ending?             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Dec 22, 2020 3:07 am A new Shin Eva poster in this tweet by Megumi Ogata (Shinji's voice actor) got me thinking about the art style of Shin Eva. tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) I mean, this is a far more sketch-heavy and weird, low-detail way of drawing the Eva characters, close to Episodes 25 and 26 of the original NGE but still not quite the same, and the ones further in the background are particularly hard to recognize. And we did get a brief glimpse of something similar to that in the piano sequence between Shinji and Kaworu in 3.0, the bizarre fairy-tale like imagery of white stallions prancing against a sky-like background. That and the general feeling that this is meant to represent some kind of ending makes me wonder if the art style later in the movie is going to be deliberately reminiscent of Episodes 25 and 26. Those two TV episodes were completed in a mad rush while incorporating that deep character analysis into the story, hence their rather unique appearance, but it appears that Anno may be invoking that for deliberate artistic purposes this time.

[]: Re: Shin Eva: A new art style towards the ending?             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Dec 22, 2020 4:42 am For what it's worth, I remember a "rumor" that said the film sould have an art style close to Dragon Ball Broly. So far nothing like that. It probably holds no basis. But I think you're onto something expecting experimentation with art style. There was also this khara animator saying that not since his student days was he reminded this much of how interesting animation is. Khara shared his tweet too. We had live action in EoE, maybe here we'll stay with animation but do all sorts of things with it. In NGE I'd say the art style stayed the same, but at the end we got see different phases of production. Well there's the part where Shinji runs into Rei amd they're chibi lol.

[]: Re: Shin Eva: A new art style towards the ending?             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Tue Dec 22, 2020 7:12 am I think it’s just an artistic style that Mahiro Maeda wanted to do. This “sketch-heavy” appearance style is nothing new: EVANGELION:3.0+1.0 first poster  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Shin Eva: A new art style towards the ending?             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Tue Dec 22, 2020 8:43 am ^26 is my favorite NGE episode, I've never understood the hate towards EoTV. If we do get some different art styles in Shin, that would be quite awesome!

[]: Re: Shin Eva: A new art style towards the ending?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 22, 2020 1:24 pm That's wasn't an official poster but a sketch one of the staff made for the staff screening of the movie tho. There was probably not the time or indeed the need for it to be more than a sketch nor is it even and official promo material, so I highly doubt its indicative of the actual movie

[]: Re: Shin Eva: A new art style towards the ending?             Quote Post by ErgoProxy » Tue Dec 22, 2020 1:29 pm Hah, I thought the same. But then I recalled this song , which fits the mood of the poster very well: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Shin Eva: A new art style towards the ending?             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Thu Dec 24, 2020 9:32 am Zusuchan wrote: I've never understood the hate towards EoTV.

[]: Re: Shin Eva: A new art style towards the ending?             Quote Post by dzzthink » Thu Dec 24, 2020 10:45 am Studio Khara have time now to reflect on the film, do some sketches and post informal social media content. The poster is probably a representation of all those involved finishing the last day of work and finally being able pat each other on the back, stand back and marvel at the Eva in the background.

[]: Re: Shin Eva: A new art style towards the ending?             Quote Post by khronos » Fri Dec 25, 2020 4:20 pm kuribo-04#903062 wrote: [...] Well there's the part where Shinji runs into Rei amd they're chibi lol.

And we did get a brief glimpse of something similar to that in the piano sequence between Shinji and Kaworu in 3.0, the bizarre fairy-tale like imagery of white stallions prancing against a sky-like background. That and the general feeling that this is meant to represent some kind of ending makes me wonder if the art style later in the movie is going to be deliberately reminiscent of Episodes 25 and 26. Those two TV episodes were completed in a mad rush while incorporating that deep character analysis into the story, hence their rather unique appearance, but it appears that Anno may be invoking that for deliberate artistic purposes this time.

In NGE I'd say the art style stayed the same, but at the end we got see different phases of production. Well there's the part where Shinji runs into Rei amd they're chibi lol.
